_readme: Canonical component-version registry for the self-improving eval/smoke/checkpoint stack.
Any change to a file under a component's `paths` must touch that component's entry in the
same change: bump `version` (new history entry, newest first) or append a same-version
history entry whose note starts with 'no-bump:' explaining why the change is behavior-neutral.
`paths` are repo-relative; a trailing '/' claims a directory prefix; longest prefix wins.
Staleness ordering is the position in `history` (newest first), never string comparison.
New ids and future bumps use monotonic v1, v2, ...; pre-existing native encodings are kept.

E618: _binding_check no longer falls back to the regex-based Gate.REFERENCES gate for structural (non-runtime-syntax) sources -- that fallback treated bare object-literal property keys (e.g. `src:` in a typed-array item `{src: ..., alt: ...}`) as unresolved variable references, permanently failing binding_correctness (reason `reference_graph_invalid`) for any correctly produced typed-array-of-objects prediction. The official-parser-derived unresolved/orphaned checks plus the $state/Query/Mutation dependency-graph reachability pass (already used for runtime sources, a safe no-op for structural sources) now run unconditionally instead.

initial registration; SLM-228 RLRG0-01 RL-readiness reward-variance gate stress test: exercises the real assess_rl_readiness function against a battery of reward-sample arms to check whether its mechanical variance>0 requirement is gameable by degenerate reward diversity

initial registration; SLM-229 RLRG0-02 RL-readiness declared-vs-actual suite-size gate stress test: exercises the real assess_rl_readiness / assert_rl_ready functions against a battery of declared-vs-actual rico_held suite-size arms to check whether the mechanical max(actual, declared) computation is gameable by a self-reported suite_sizes metadata claim decoupled from the actually-evaluated suite

initial registration; SLM-230 SPV0-04 plan-factor oracle-substitution ceiling matrix: exercises the real PlanOracleSubstitutor / PlanSeedBuilder / OpenUISemanticPlanCompiler against factor-subset (roles/topology/bindings) oracle arms on the SLM-144 fixture corpus, supplying the factor-wise downstream-ceiling evidence SLM-145's authorization gate and the SLM-160 SPV program disposition both named as the missing next step

initial registration; SLM-231 SPV0-05 RoleSlot cardinality dead-field consumption probe: exercises the real PlanOracleSubstitutor / PlanSeedBuilder / OpenUISemanticPlanCompiler with a harness-local candidate cardinality derivation on the roles+topology oracle arm, diffing per-record seed output with/without cardinality populated to test the SLM-145/SLM-230 cardinality gap's consumer side (extractor producer-side gap already confirmed by SLM-230)

initial registration; SLM-232 PEL0-01 preference build-pairs eval-holdout leakage gate stress test: exercises the real build_test_data / collect_pairs_with_generator / write_pairs / find_leakage / load_train_fingerprints functions to test whether the preference phase enforces the documented 'never train on eval-feedback holdouts' invariant the way test-data enforces train/eval disjointness

initial registration; SLM-233 DTL0-01 distillation P2 trace-selection eval-holdout leakage gate stress test: exercises the real build_test_data / DecodeTraceRecorder.finalize / TraceStore.append / select_traces functions to test whether the P2 self-distillation selection stage enforces the documented 'selection data stays disjoint from frozen evals; never train on held-out benchmark traces' invariant the way test-data enforces train/eval disjointness

initial registration; SLM-234 CKM0-01 TIES-vs-average merge signal recovery under synthetic parameter interference: exercises the real harness_core.lineage.merge.merge_checkpoints (method=average, method=ties) on synthetic parent+sibling checkpoints with a known signal/noise ground truth to test whether TIES-Merging's trim + sign-election + disjoint-merge mechanism recovers a consensus update direction/magnitude at least as well as naive averaging as sibling interference rises

initial registration; SLM-235 AEP0-01 annotation export pairing-mechanism consistency probe: exercises the real FileAnnotationStore.persist / persist_annotation / maybe_append_preference_pair (live) and export_to_preference_pairs / write_pairs (batch) functions over synthetic multi-flip annotation sessions to test whether running the documented `slm annotations export` CLI against the shared default pairs path silently discards preference pairs the live FastAPI /api/annotate path had already durably persisted there

initial registration; SLM-236 SSR0-01 structural-similarity literal-content robustness probe: exercises the real structural_similarity / _component_multiset functions (harnesses/model_build/eval_runner.py) and the real score_openui RL-reward contract (integrations/openui_rl.py) over synthetic grammar-valid OpenUI document pairs that share identical DSL structure but differ only in one leaf literal's text, to test whether ordinary literal-text punctuation or a component-shaped substring silently lowers a nominally style-agnostic structural similarity score and its downstream RL composite reward term

initial registration; SLM-237 PCR0-01 placeholder-contract literal-content robustness probe: exercises the real extract_placeholders (dsl/placeholders.py), _contract_precision / _contract_recall / _placeholder_fidelity (harnesses/model_build/eval_runner.py), and score_openui RL-reward contract (integrations/openui_rl.py) over synthetic grammar-valid OpenUI document pairs that share identical DSL structure but differ only in leaf-literal text, to test whether an incidental placeholder-shaped literal mention can spuriously lower contract precision, and -- more severely -- award full false credit on all three contract metrics and the RL reward's placeholder_fidelity term for a prediction that omits its one real contract placeholder entirely

initial registration; SLM-238 GAE0-01 gradient-accumulation equivalence sweep: follows up on the single-run iter-telemetry-gradaccum2-20260715 / iter-telemetry-effective-batch-20260715 probes with a multi-seed, matched-init/LR/data comparison of the real model_build train loop's grad_accum_steps=2 (micro-batch 4) arm against direct batch_size=8 (grad_accum=1) training, checking both final-loss closeness against a pre-registered relative tolerance and the accel.grad_accum / accel.effective_batch_size telemetry accounting; positive mechanism-characterization harness, not a metric/gate-gap probe

initial registration; SLM-240 LRS0-01 learning-rate schedule gap probe: instruments the real model_build train loop's live optimizer.step() calls (monkeypatch spy on torch.optim.AdamW.step / MuonHybrid.step, restore-after, never altering control flow) across adamw and muon_hybrid arms and multiple seeds to confirm that ModelBuildConfig exposes no warmup/decay knob and train_loop.py never adjusts the optimizer's parameter-group lr after construction, and that per-step metrics.jsonl rows never log the applied lr either; gap-finding probe over real production code, not a metric/gate change and no line of train_loop.py, config.py, or muon.py is modified

initial registration; SLM-239 CPM0-01 checkpoint-migrate output-head vocab-remap corruption probe: exercises the real, unmodified migrate_twotower_checkpoint (models/checkpoint_migrate.py) end to end -- construct a tiny TwoTowerModel, save a checkpoint, migrate it against a permuted train-records set that reproduces the same vocab size with a different first-occurrence token order, reload the on-disk migrated checkpoint -- across 5 seeds x {tie_output_embedding=True, False}, checking whether the output head's per-key remap actually covers denoiser.lm_head.weight the way the docstring promises for denoiser.tok.weight; gap-finding probe over real production migration code, not a metric/gate change and no line of checkpoint_migrate.py is modified
